title: Agent Greeting Upon Connection
description:
The agent should greet the player when they connect to Minecraft. Adds world-building and immersion.

## Implementation Plan (MVP)

1. Add `GreetingMessage` property to `ChatOptions`:
   - `public string? GreetingMessage { get; init; }` — null = no greeting
   - Configure in appsettings.json: `"Agent": { "Chat": { "GreetingMessage": "Hi! I'm ready to help." } }`
2. In `AgentBackgroundService`, track `_greeted` flag (reset on disconnect)
3. On first `StatusEvent` after WebSocket connects AND bot spawn event received:
   - If `_greeted` is false AND GreetingMessage is non-null AND no active goal → dispatch Chat tool with greeting
   - Set `_greeted = true`
4. Guard: don't greet if a goal is already active (agent is busy)

## Future Extension: AgentProfile Integration

AgentProfile.cs (Agent.Personality) has `Name`, `Backstory`, `VoiceStyle` fields but is dead code — never injected into the LLM pipeline. In a future sprint, AgentProfile should be:
- Registered in DI
- Injected into `LlmChatInterpreter.BuildSystemPrompt` (personality section in prompt)
- Used as the source for the greeting message (derive from Name + Backstory)
This is deferred because the current personality model isn't dynamic enough to warrant the injection yet.

## Files
- Agent.Planning/Llm/ChatOptions.cs — add GreetingMessage property
- WebUI.Blazor/AgentBackgroundService.cs — greeting dispatch on connect/spawn
- WebUI.Blazor/appsettings.json — configure greeting
- Future: Agent.Personality/AgentProfile.cs — extend and wire into LLM pipeline
